sections.paragraph:
(i.q. Arab. حَرَّانُ a place dried up, or parched with the sun), [Haran], pr.n.-

(1) of a town of Mesopotamia, called in Gr. and Lat. Κάῤῥαι, Carrœ, Arab. and Syr. حَرَّانُ, ܚܳܪܢܳ afterwards celebrated for the defeat of Crassus; Gen 11:31 12:5 27:43 2Ki 19:12 and Eze 27:23 (in this passage, J. D. Michaëlis, who follows a false hypothesis as to this whole verse, understands some other city in Arabia Felix); see Gol. ad Alferg. p. 249; Schult. Ind. Geogr. v. Charræ; J. D. Michaëlis, Suppl. p. 930.-(2) of a man, 1Ch 2:46.
